message:
feat(web): create standardized Badge component with variants

- Add Badge.tsx with variant props (default, info, warning, success, error, subtle)
- Add size props (default, small, tiny)
- Add convenience exports: InfoBadge, WarningBadge, SuccessBadge, ErrorBadge, SubtleBadge
- Support interactive (clickable) badges and pulse animation
- Update Model3DView to use Badge components
- Update SimulationView to use semantic badges (ErrorBadge, SuccessBadge)
- Update Header to use Badge and WarningBadge with pulse
- Update ComparisonOverlay to use Badge

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"phage_explorer-jvl7","content_hash":"6b61b7e776efba30049c39fb735530c793bf52967dc0be48baada54ce44a7225","title":"Feature: Touch-Optimized Control Palette","description":"","notes":"# Feature: Touch-Optimized Control Palette

## Problem Statement

The web app currently requires keyboard shortcuts to access all advanced features. This makes the app inaccessible to:
- Mobile users (no physical keyboard)
- Tablet users (on-screen keyboard blocks view)
- Touchscreen laptop users
- Users with motor disabilities affecting keyboard use
- Casual users who won't memorize shortcuts

## Design Philosophy

The control palette should follow mobile-first design principles:
1. **Thumb-reachable**: Primary controls in bottom 1/3 of screen
2. **Progressive disclosure**: Simple options first, advanced on demand
3. **Touch targets**: Minimum 44x44px tap targets (Apple HIG)
4. **Visual feedback**: Clear pressed/active states
5. **Gesture-aware**: Support for swipe, pinch, long-press

## Proposed Architecture

### Layer 1: Floating Action Button (FAB)
A persistent button in the bottom-right corner that opens the action drawer.

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                     │
│       [Main Content Area]           │
│                                     │
│                                     │
│                                ⚙️   │ ← FAB
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Layer 2: Action Drawer
A bottom sheet that slides up with categorized quick actions.

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│       [Main Content]                │
├─────────────────────────────────────┤
│  ═══════════════════  (drag handle) │
│                                     │
│  VIEW          ANALYSIS      3D     │
│  ┌──────┐     ┌──────┐   ┌──────┐  │
│  │DNA   │     │GC    │   │Ball  │  │
│  │      │     │Skew  │   │Stick │  │
│  └──────┘     └──────┘   └──────┘  │
│  ┌──────┐     ┌──────┐   ┌──────┐  │
│  │Amino │     │Kmer  │   │Ribbon│  │
│  │Acids │     │      │   │      │  │
│  └──────┘     └──────┘   └──────┘  │
│                                     │
│  [⋯ More]                           │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Layer 3: Full Feature Modal
Accessed via "More" button or long-press on FAB.

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────┐
│  ╳  All Features                    │
├─────────────────────────────────────┤
│  🔍 Search features...              │
├─────────────────────────────────────┤
│  ▼ Navigation                       │
│    • Next phage (j)                 │
│    • Previous phage (k)             │
│    • Go to start (Home)             │
│    • Go to end (End)                │
├─────────────────────────────────────┤
│  ▼ View Options                     │
│    • DNA view (v)                   │
│    • Amino acid view (v)            │
│    • Cycle reading frame (f)        │
│    • Toggle diff mode (d)           │
├─────────────────────────────────────┤
│  ▼ Analysis Overlays                │
│    • GC Skew (g)                    │
│    • Complexity (x)                 │
│    • ...                            │
└─────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Component Breakdown

### 1. FloatingActionButton (FAB)
- Position: fixed, bottom-right
- Icon: Gear or menu icon
- States: normal, pressed, expanded
- Haptic feedback on tap

### 2. ActionDrawer
- Uses CSS transforms for smooth animation
- Drag-to-dismiss with velocity detection
- Backdrop blur/dim when open
- Categories from keyboard shortcut registry

### 3. FullFeatureModal
- Fuzzy search across all actions
- Grouped by category
- Shows keyboard shortcuts as hints
- Touch-optimized list items

## State Management

```typescript
interface ControlPaletteState {
  fabPosition: { x: number; y: number };
  drawerOpen: boolean;
  drawerHeight: 'collapsed' | 'half' | 'full';
  modalOpen: boolean;
  recentActions: string[];  // For quick access
}
```

## Accessibility Requirements

- ARIA labels on all interactive elements
- Focus management when drawer/modal opens
- Support for external keyboard when connected
- Reduced motion support (instant transitions)
- High contrast mode support

## Dependencies

- phage_explorer-tp98: FAB implementation
- phage_explorer-wd9q: Action drawer
- phage_explorer-j4ra: Full modal
- phage_explorer-zuiv: Gesture support

## Definition of Done

- [ ] FAB visible and functional on mobile
- [ ] Action drawer provides quick access to common features
- [ ] Full modal lists ALL available features
- [ ] Works on iOS Safari, Android Chrome
- [ ] Touch targets meet 44x44px minimum
- [ ] Smooth 60fps animations","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-09T11:25:49.692639-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-09T11:32:54.706763-05:00","source_repo":".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-jvl7","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-jjq5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-09T11:26:24.380857-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"phage_explorer-k3l5","content_hash":"23b391e9a36210349c579fcc79d5c39a81f30663c5adb8cf46740c588b44789e","title":"Add functional group visualization in 3D","description":"","notes":"# Add Functional Group Visualization in 3D

## Problem Statement

In large molecular structures, atoms appear as "a big tangled mass" without clear visual organization. Functional groups (collections of atoms with specific biological/chemical purpose) should be visually distinguished to help users understand molecular architecture.

## What Are Functional Groups?

In proteins and nucleic acids, functional groups are recognizable substructures:

### Amino Acid Side Chains
- **Aromatic rings** (Phe, Tyr, Trp): 6-membered carbon rings
- **Sulfur bridges** (Cys-Cys): Disulfide bonds
- **Charged groups** (Lys, Arg, Asp, Glu): Ionizable at physiological pH
- **Hydroxyl groups** (Ser, Thr, Tyr): -OH groups

### Nucleic Acid Components
- **Phosphate backbone**: PO4 groups in DNA/RNA
- **Sugar rings**: Ribose (RNA) or deoxyribose (DNA)
- **Bases**: Adenine, Guanine, Cytosine, Thymine/Uracil

### Prosthetic Groups
- **Heme groups**: Iron-containing porphyrin
- **Metal centers**: Zinc fingers, calcium binding sites

## Proposed Visualization Approaches

### Option 1: Outline/Highlight Method
Draw a subtle outline or glow around atoms belonging to the same functional group:

```typescript
interface FunctionalGroup {
  type: 'aromatic' | 'disulfide' | 'phosphate' | 'heme' | 'metal_center';
  atomIndices: number[];
  color: Color;
  highlightStyle: 'outline' | 'glow' | 'halo';
}
```

### Option 2: Bounding Shape Method
Render translucent bounding shapes (ellipsoids, boxes) around functional groups:

```typescript
function buildFunctionalGroupBounds(
  atoms: AtomRecord[],
  groups: FunctionalGroup[]
): Group {
  const meshes = groups.map(group => {
    const groupAtoms = group.atomIndices.map(i => atoms[i]);
    const centroid = computeCentroid(groupAtoms);
    const bounds = computeBoundingEllipsoid(groupAtoms);
    return createTranslucentEllipsoid(centroid, bounds, group.color);
  });
  return new Group().add(...meshes);
}
```

### Option 3: Connection Line Method
Draw lines connecting atoms in the same functional group:

```typescript
function buildFunctionalGroupConnections(
  atoms: AtomRecord[],
  groups: FunctionalGroup[]
): Group {
  // Draw thin lines between atoms in each group
  // Different colors per group type
}
```

## Detection Algorithms

### Aromatic Ring Detection
```typescript
function detectAromaticRings(atoms: AtomRecord[], bonds: Bond[]): number[][] {
  // Find 6-membered carbon rings
  // Check planarity (dihedral angles)
  // Return atom indices for each ring
}
```

### Disulfide Bond Detection
```typescript
function detectDisulfideBonds(atoms: AtomRecord[]): [number, number][] {
  // Find pairs of sulfur atoms (from Cys residues)
  // Check distance (typical S-S bond: 2.0-2.1 Å)
}
```

### Phosphate Group Detection
```typescript
function detectPhosphateGroups(atoms: AtomRecord[]): number[][] {
  // Find P atoms bonded to 4 O atoms
  // Return phosphate group atom indices
}
```

## UI Integration

Add a toggle or dropdown to enable functional group highlighting:

```tsx
<button onClick={() => setShowFunctionalGroups(!showFunctionalGroups)}>
  Show Groups
</button>

<select value={highlightStyle} onChange={setHighlightStyle}>
  <option value="none">None</option>
  <option value="outline">Outline</option>
  <option value="glow">Glow</option>
  <option value="bounds">Bounding Shapes</option>
</select>
```

## Performance Considerations

- Functional group detection should be cached per structure
- Highlight rendering should use instanced meshes
- Consider LOD: hide details at far zoom levels

## Dependencies

- Requires improved atom coloring (phage_explorer-qm08) to be complete first
- Group detection needs accurate bond information

## Definition of Done

- [ ] Aromatic rings visually distinguishable
- [ ] Disulfide bridges highlighted
- [ ] Phosphate groups (if present) highlighted
- [ ] Toggle to enable/disable group visualization
- [ ] Performance acceptable on structures with 10k+ atoms","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-09T11:25:47.65109-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T13:56:36.286197498-06:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T13:56:36.286197498-06:00","source_repo":".","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"phage_explorer-k3l5","depends_on_id":"phage_explorer-qm08","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-09T11:26:23.690228-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
